Get Your Solar Proposal

Commercial solar built around operations

Commercial Solar With a Business Case

Turn suitable roof or land area into a long-term energy asset with a system shaped around operating loads and business priorities.

Request a Commercial AssessmentSee How It Works
Operating-load review
Roof and land options
Capital-planning clarity
Home/Commercial Solar With a Business Case

Treat solar like the long-term business asset it is.

Commercial solar needs more than a panel count. We bring the facility, operating schedule, electrical demand, roof lifecycle and project priorities into one practical assessment.

  • Annual and interval electricity use
  • Daytime demand and operating schedules
  • Roof structure, lifecycle and site constraints
  • Electrical capacity and interconnection needs
  • Construction access and disruption planning
  • Portfolio reporting and sustainability objectives
Start Your Property Review

Clarity where the project decisions matter.

A strong solar project connects the energy profile, the property and the path to installation.

01

Facility-first design

We compare the available roof or land with the facility's load profile, operating realities and long-term property plans.

02

A visible business case

Key assumptions, production estimates and project variables are organized so decision-makers can assess the opportunity clearly.

03

Decision-ready planning

Technical requirements, approval steps and implementation considerations are presented in a format that supports internal review.

A complete commercial review, not a generic estimate.

Every facility has a different relationship between roof area, electricity demand and project constraints. The review should be rigorous enough to support the next business decision.

  • Load and tariff profile
  • Rooftop versus ground mount
  • Roof and electrical capacity
  • Construction sequencing
  • Current program eligibility
  • Monitoring and reporting needs

Production, savings, incentives and timelines vary by site. A property-specific assessment is required.

A clear path from assessment to activation.

A structured route from preliminary fit to an installation plan your team can evaluate.

01

Qualify the opportunity

Review usage, site data, priorities and project constraints.

02

Develop the concept

Compare layouts, production assumptions and technical fit.

03

Build the project plan

Coordinate approvals, capital timing and implementation details.

04

Deliver and monitor

Install, commission and establish a clear monitoring view.

Questions about commercial solar

Straight answers help you decide whether the next step makes sense for your property.

Can solar installation be planned without shutting down our operations?+

Often the work can be staged around operating hours and access requirements, but electrical tie-ins may require a planned interruption. The schedule should identify disruption risks before construction.

Is an older commercial roof suitable for solar?+

Roof structure, membrane condition, remaining service life, drainage and warranty requirements all need review. If major roof work is approaching, coordinating both projects may reduce future removal costs.

What electricity information is needed for a commercial assessment?+

Twelve months of bills are a useful start. Interval data, demand charges, operating schedules, planned equipment and information about multiple meters can make the analysis more representative.

Can one proposal cover several buildings or utility meters?+

Yes, but each building and meter may have different loads, roof conditions and interconnection constraints. A portfolio review should show which sites offer the strongest practical fit.

How is commercial solar performance monitored?+

Monitoring can show system production, equipment status and historical trends. Reporting needs should be discussed early if facilities, finance or sustainability teams require shared access or portfolio-level views.

Are commercial tax incentives or grants guaranteed?+

No. Programs, eligibility and tax treatment can change and depend on the organization and project. Current official rules and advice from qualified tax and financial professionals should guide decisions.

Your next step

Turn Sunlight Into a Smarter Energy Plan

Share your electricity usage and property details. We’ll organize the information needed to shape a clear solar proposal.
